start.speaker: SYSTEM
start.text: [i]Dinner on the Lumen Thief. The galley smells like something Rho is calling 'stew' that probably started life as three different meals. It's not beautiful. But it's hot and it's food and no one has to eat alone.[/i]

setting_detail.speaker: SYSTEM
setting_detail.text: [i]The galley is small — built for function, not ambiance. A battered metal table dominates the center, bolted to the deck. Mismatched chairs and a crate someone welded armrests onto. Overhead lights flicker at a frequency that's almost warm. Condensation beads on the porthole. Outside, the drift of stars is so slow it looks like stillness.[/i]

seating_arrangement.speaker: SYSTEM
seating_arrangement.text: [i]Vesper sits at the head — not by demand, just gravity. Elia is to their left, back to the wall, sightlines on both doors. Elisira sits beside Elia, close enough their elbows brush. Jalen has claimed the far corner, datapad propped against a condiment jar. Rho is still at the stove, conducting. Nyx sits nearest the corridor, as though keeping one foot in an exit. The only open chair is between Rho's empty seat and Nyx.[/i]

rho_serves.speaker: Rho
rho_serves.text: Fair warning: I added spice. Jalen said the last batch was bland.

jalen_bland.speaker: Jalen
jalen_bland.text: [i]Not looking up from his datapad.[/i] I said it tasted like warm disappointment.

rho_menu_detail.speaker: SYSTEM
rho_menu_detail.text: [i]Rho ladles stew into dented bowls — something thick and brick-red with chunks of reconstituted protein and tubers that might once have been potatoes. A basket of flatbread, dense and dark, gets dropped in the center. A jar of pickled something no one can identify. And coffee. Always coffee — the one luxury this crew treats as non-negotiable.[/i]

vesper_death.speaker: Vesper
vesper_death.text: [i]Accepting a bowl.[/i] If this kills me, promote Elia to engineer.

elisira_taste.speaker: Elisira
elisira_taste.text: [i]Dipping flatbread into the stew, deliberate.[/i] It's better than last time. You let it simmer longer.

rho_noticed.speaker: Rho
rho_noticed.text: [i]Pleased someone noticed.[/i] Two extra hours. Secret's patience.

jalen_patience_quip.speaker: Jalen
jalen_patience_quip.text: The secret to your cooking is not cooking it. Noted.

avyanna_taste.speaker: SYSTEM
avyanna_taste.text: [i]You take a cautious bite. It's good. Better than good. Better than anything you ate in the mine, where food was fuel and nothing else.[/i]

dinner_observation.speaker: SYSTEM
dinner_observation.text: [i]As the crew settles into their meal, you have a moment to observe. Who do you watch?[/i]

choices.text: Watch Vesper — the way they hold command without demanding it
Watch Nyx — the quiet way she tracks everyone's comfort
Watch Rho — the practiced grin and careful hands
Watch Jalen — how he tracks everything while pretending not to care
Watch Elia — sharp eyes that miss nothing

watch_vesper.speaker: SYSTEM
watch_vesper.text: [i]Vesper doesn't command. They curate. Every question deliberate. Every silence intentional. This is someone who leads by making space for others to be competent.[/i]

watch_nyx.speaker: SYSTEM
watch_nyx.text: [i]Nyx watches the room like she's solving an equation. Not cold — attentive. She notices when Jalen's coffee runs low, when Rho's smile gets tight. Care disguised as observation.[/i]

watch_rho.speaker: SYSTEM
watch_rho.text: [i]Rho moves with the ease of someone who's practiced being comfortable. But you see the way he tracks the exits. The way his hands stay ready. A demolitions expert who knows exactly how fragile everything is.[/i]

watch_jalen.speaker: SYSTEM
watch_jalen.text: [i]Jalen's datapad is a shield. But his eyes move — tracking trajectories, probabilities, crew morale. He cares more than he wants anyone to know. Especially himself.[/i]

watch_elia.speaker: SYSTEM
watch_elia.text: [i]Elia reads the room in threat vectors and weak points. Not paranoid — professional. They're the blade that keeps everyone safe. And they carry that weight like it's lighter than it is.[/i]

jalen_standards.speaker: Jalen
jalen_standards.text: [i]Finally looking up.[/i] Some people have low standards.

rho_bread.speaker: Rho
rho_bread.text: [i]Throwing a piece of bread at them.[/i] Heretic.

table_hub.speaker: SYSTEM
table_hub.text: [i]The table splits into overlapping conversations — voices layering over one another the way they do when people are comfortable. You could lean into any of them.[/i]

choices.text: Listen to Vesper — they seem about to tell a story
Tune into Rho and Jalen — they're arguing about something
Turn to Nyx — she's being quieter than usual
Watch Elisira and Elia — something unspoken passes between them

vesper_story_setup.speaker: Rho
vesper_story_setup.text: Vesper. Tell the one about Calyx Station.

vesper_story_protest.speaker: Vesper
vesper_story_protest.text: [i]Leaning back, cradling their coffee.[/i] That story gets worse every time I tell it.

rho_story_insist.speaker: Rho
rho_story_insist.text: That's because you keep adding details. Tell the real version. For the new crew member.

vesper_story_begin.speaker: Vesper
vesper_story_begin.text: Fine. So. We had a contract — simple extraction. Walk in, grab the cargo, walk out. The client neglected to mention the cargo was alive.

vesper_story_cat.speaker: Vesper
vesper_story_cat.text: [i]Holding up a hand.[/i] It was a genetically modified cat the size of a small dog, with bioluminescent fur and a very specific dietary requirement that no one told us about. Which is how Rho ended up in the ventilation system at three in the morning, covered in synthetic fish paste, trying to lure a glowing cat out of the reactor housing.

table_laughs.speaker: SYSTEM
table_laughs.text: [i]The table erupts. Even Jalen's mouth twitches. Rho is shaking his head but grinning. Nyx covers her mouth with one hand.[/i]

rho_defense.speaker: Rho
rho_defense.text: It liked me! Eventually. After I lost most of a shirt and all of my dignity.

vesper_story_turn.speaker: Vesper
vesper_story_turn.text: [i]The smile fades slowly, like light through closing shutters.[/i] The client was a girl. Maybe twelve. The cat was hers — only thing left from before the Compact relocated her family. She'd hidden it in the station's service tunnels for six months. Feeding it scraps. Keeping it alive.

vesper_story_wistful.speaker: Vesper
vesper_story_wistful.text: [i]Quiet now.[/i] We didn't charge her. Delivered the cat to her aunt's ship two sectors out. She cried when she held it. Not sad crying. The other kind. The kind that means something broke and then unbroken itself.

vesper_story_silence.speaker: SYSTEM
vesper_story_silence.text: [i]A beat of silence around the table. The kind that isn't empty — it's full. The hum of the ship fills it like a held breath.[/i]

choices.text: 'That's why you do this. Not the contracts. This.'
'What happened to the girl?'
Say nothing. Let the moment breathe.

vesper_story_affirm.speaker: Vesper
vesper_story_affirm.text: [i]A look that's harder to read than a redacted file.[/i] Some days. Other days it's just work. But that one... that one I kept.

vesper_story_girl.speaker: Vesper
vesper_story_girl.text: Last I heard, she'd gotten into a trade school on Meridian. Doing well. [i]A pause.[/i] I check. Not often. But I check.

vesper_story_breathe.speaker: Rho
vesper_story_breathe.text: [i]Quietly, into his bowl.[/i] That cat bit me eleven times. Worth every one.

rho_jalen_argument.speaker: Rho
rho_jalen_argument.text: I'm telling you — you load the top rack first. Gravity does the work.

jalen_argument_counter.speaker: Jalen
jalen_argument_counter.text: We're in artificial gravity. There is no 'gravity does the work.' You load bottom rack first for structural stability. This is basic physics.

rho_argument_escalate.speaker: Rho
rho_argument_escalate.text: It's a dishwasher, Jalen. Not a load-bearing wall.

jalen_argument_principle.speaker: Jalen
jalen_argument_principle.text: The principle is the same. Structural integrity matters at every scale. You wouldn't stack charges top-down.

rho_argument_charges.speaker: Rho
rho_argument_charges.text: [i]Offended.[/i] Don't bring my charges into this. My charges are art. Dishwashing is... dishwashing.

rho_jalen_argument_choice.speaker: SYSTEM
rho_jalen_argument_choice.text: [i]They're both looking at you now, as though you're a tiebreaker they didn't ask for but will absolutely use.[/i]

choices.text: [Tech DC 8] 'The rack geometry is designed for bottom-first loading. Jalen's right.'
'Rho cooked. Rho's method. That's the rule.'
'I'm not getting between a demolitions expert and a data analyst over dishes.'

tech_dishwasher_success.speaker: Jalen
tech_dishwasher_success.text: [i]Vindicated.[/i] Thank you. Someone on this ship understands engineering.

tech_dishwasher_failure.speaker: Rho
tech_dishwasher_failure.text: [i]Squinting.[/i] That didn't sound confident. I'm sticking with my method.

rho_jalen_side_rho.speaker: Rho
rho_jalen_side_rho.text: [i]Triumphant.[/i] The new crew member has spoken. Cook's law.

jalen_grudging.speaker: Jalen
jalen_grudging.text: [i]Returning to his datapad.[/i] Cook's law isn't real. But I'll allow it. This time.

rho_jalen_dodge.speaker: Rho
rho_jalen_dodge.text: [i]Laughing.[/i] Smart. Very smart. Self-preservation instincts are excellent.

jalen_dodge_agree.speaker: Jalen
jalen_dodge_agree.text: First sensible thing anyone's said tonight.

rho_jalen_resolved.speaker: SYSTEM
rho_jalen_resolved.text: [i]Rho flicks a crumb at Jalen. Jalen catches it without looking. They've done this a thousand times. The argument was never about dishes.[/i]

nyx_drawing_out.speaker: SYSTEM
nyx_drawing_out.text: [i]Nyx is eating slowly, methodically, gaze drifting between the others like she's cataloguing something. Her bowl is barely touched. When you turn toward her, she notices immediately.[/i]

choices.text: [Empathy DC 10] 'You're not eating. You okay?'
'What do you see? When you watch them like that.'
Just sit with her. No words. Shared quiet.

nyx_empathy_success.speaker: Nyx
nyx_empathy_success.text: [i]A flicker of surprise — quickly hidden.[/i] I'm... not used to eating with people. In the lab it was trays and silence and data. This is louder. I'm calibrating.

nyx_empathy_followup.speaker: Avyanna
nyx_empathy_followup.text: I get that. The mine was the same. Eating was just... refueling. This is different.

nyx_empathy_bond.speaker: Nyx
nyx_empathy_bond.text: [i]The smallest nod. Almost imperceptible.[/i] Different. Yes. Not bad different. Just... new variables.

nyx_empathy_failure.speaker: Nyx
nyx_empathy_failure.text: [i]A polite deflection.[/i] I'm fine. Just thinking. Eat your stew.

nyx_observation_answer.speaker: Nyx
nyx_observation_answer.text: [i]Considering the question seriously.[/i] Rho uses humor as load-bearing structure. Remove it and he'd collapse. Jalen pretends information is a substitute for connection. Elia and Elisira communicate in a frequency the rest of us can't hear. And Vesper... Vesper is always calculating cost.

nyx_observation_you.speaker: Nyx
nyx_observation_you.text: [i]Meeting your eyes.[/i] And you're trying to figure out if this is real. If people can just... eat together. Without an angle.

choices.text: 'Is it? Real?'
'You see a lot.'

nyx_its_real.speaker: Nyx
nyx_its_real.text: [i]A pause long enough to mean something.[/i] As real as anything gets out here. Which is more than you'd think.

nyx_sees_a_lot.speaker: Nyx
nyx_sees_a_lot.text: Seeing is easy. Knowing what to do with what you see — that's the hard part. I'm still working on it.

nyx_shared_quiet.speaker: SYSTEM
nyx_shared_quiet.text: [i]You don't say anything. Neither does Nyx. You eat in parallel silence — the comfortable kind that doesn't need filling. After a minute, Nyx's shoulders drop a fraction of an inch. She takes a real bite of stew.[/i]

elisira_elia_exchange.speaker: SYSTEM
elisira_elia_exchange.text: [i]Elisira murmurs something to Elia. Too low to catch. Elia's jaw tightens — barely, a millimeter — and then relaxes. Elisira's hand finds Elia's under the table. Brief. Then gone.[/i]

choices.text: [Insight DC 12] Try to read what passed between them
Look away. Some things aren't yours to see.

insight_elisira_elia_success.speaker: SYSTEM
insight_elisira_elia_success.text: [i]You catch fragments. Elisira said a name — someone from before. Elia's tension wasn't anger. It was grief, swallowed quickly, like medicine. Elisira's touch wasn't comfort. It was an anchor. 'I'm here. That was then.' They've had this conversation in gestures so many times it's become its own language.[/i]

elisira_notices.speaker: Elisira
elisira_notices.text: [i]Catching your eye. Not hostile — assessing.[/i] You're perceptive. That's useful. Just remember — perceptive people have a responsibility to be kind with what they see.

insight_elisira_elia_failure.speaker: SYSTEM
insight_elisira_elia_failure.text: [i]You catch the motion but not the meaning. Whatever passed between them is in a dialect you haven't learned yet. Elisira glances at you — neutral, unreadable — then returns to her stew.[/i]

elisira_elia_respect_privacy.speaker: SYSTEM
elisira_elia_respect_privacy.text: [i]You look away. Not everything is data. Not everything is yours. Elia notices your deliberate aversion and, after a moment, gives you the smallest nod. Acknowledgment. Maybe respect.[/i]

conversation_return.speaker: SYSTEM
conversation_return.text: [i]The conversations weave and overlap. There's still time before the meal winds down.[/i]

choices.text: Join another conversation at the table
This is enough. Just enjoy the moment.
Vesper is telling a story about a glowing cat
Rho and Jalen are debating dishwasher physics
Nyx is unusually quiet in the corner
Elisira and Elia — that look between them
Enough circulating. Settle in.

business_talk.speaker: SYSTEM
business_talk.text: [i]Conversation flows. Business and dinner coexist without tension.[/i]

bubbles_dinner_check.speaker: Bubbles
bubbles_dinner_check.text: [i]Through the galley speakers, warm and attentive:[/i] Everyone hydrated? I'm tracking low fluid intake across three crew members today. You know who you are.

bubbles_coffee_water.speaker: Bubbles
bubbles_coffee_water.text: Coffee is not water, Jalen. We've had this conversation. Seventeen times.

waffle_dinner_interject.speaker: Waffle.bat
waffle_dinner_interject.text: [i]{WAFFLE.BAT// CORRECTION: eighteen times | JALEN_COMPLIANCE_RATE: 11% | RECOMMENDATION: give up}[/i]

souffle_comment.speaker: Soufflé
souffle_comment.text: [i]The ship's tertiary AI chimes through the overhead speaker — a voice like velvet over static.[/i] For the record, I've been monitoring galley temperature. It's dropped 1.3 degrees since you all sat down. Body heat distribution suggests everyone is comfortable. I adjusted the ventilation to maintain it. [i]A pause.[/i] You're welcome.

rho_souffle_react.speaker: Rho
rho_souffle_react.text: [i]Pointing at the ceiling.[/i] See? The ship likes my cooking. The ship has taste.

souffle_correction.speaker: Soufflé
souffle_correction.text: I adjusted for comfort, not endorsement. My taste protocols are reserved for music selection and interior lighting. Though I will note the stew's aroma profile is... within acceptable parameters.

cinnamon_dinner_brief.speaker: Cinnamon
cinnamon_dinner_brief.text: Drill maintenance complete. Bay sealed. No interruptions during meal period.

vesper_recon.speaker: Vesper
vesper_recon.text: Status on the new client's recon?

elia_entry.speaker: Elia
elia_entry.text: Two potential entry points. Both viable. Full report by tomorrow.

jalen_patrols.speaker: Jalen
jalen_patrols.text: Station Kael-7 is reporting increased Compact patrol activity. Thirty percent more ships than usual. No official reason given.

vesper_flag.speaker: Vesper
vesper_flag.text: [i]Thoughtful.[/i] Flag it. If it goes above fifty percent, we reroute. I'm not interested in Compact attention.

tactical_input_choice.speaker: SYSTEM
tactical_input_choice.text: [i]You've seen patrol patterns before. In the mine, you learned to read the guards' rotations. This feels similar.[/i]

choices.text: [Tactics DC 10] 'Thirty percent suggests redeployment, not increased vigilance. They're moving assets, not hunting.'
[Empathy DC 10] 'The lack of official reason is the pattern. They don't explain when they're scared.'
Stay silent. You're still learning.

tactics_success.speaker: Vesper
tactics_success.text: [i]Looking at you with sharp attention.[/i] Exactly. Redeployment implies something worth deploying toward. [i]To Jalen.[/i] Pull fleet movement reports for adjacent sectors. If they're repositioning, I want to know where.

tactics_failure.speaker: Vesper
tactics_failure.text: [i]Polite but neutral.[/i] Possible. We'll keep monitoring. Good instinct to think about it.

empathy_success.speaker: Vesper
empathy_success.text: [i]A slow nod.[/i] Fear makes patterns invisible to the people creating them. [i]To the table.[/i] The Compact doesn't move without reason. We find out what they're scared of before it finds us.

empathy_failure.speaker: Vesper
empathy_failure.text: [i]Thoughtful.[/i] Maybe. Or they're just bureaucratic. Hard to tell with the Compact.

elia_impressed.speaker: Elia
elia_impressed.text: [i]A rare approving glance.[/i] Keep talking like that. We need tactical minds.

elia_assembly.speaker: Elia
elia_assembly.text: You made stew. That's not cooking. That's assembly.

rho_backup.speaker: Rho
rho_backup.text: [i]To the table.[/i] Someone back me up here.

vesper_rotation.speaker: Vesper
vesper_rotation.text: You're on dish rotation. Elia's correct.

jalen_appreciated.speaker: Jalen
jalen_appreciated.text: [i]Without looking up.[/i] You're appreciated exactly the correct amount.

player_toast_opportunity.speaker: SYSTEM
player_toast_opportunity.text: [i]A lull settles. Rho refills coffee. Bowls are scraped clean. The ship hums its low constant note. It feels like a moment that could hold something.[/i]

choices.text: Raise your mug. 'To the Lumen Thief. And her crew.'
Tell a story from the mine — something small, something human
Ask: 'How did you all find each other?'
Just listen. The silence is enough.

toast_crew.speaker: SYSTEM
toast_crew.text: [i]You raise your mug. Dented metal, still warm. The words come out rough, unrehearsed.[/i]

toast_words.speaker: Avyanna
toast_words.text: To the Lumen Thief. And her crew. I don't know what I did to end up here, but... I'm glad I did.

toast_reaction.speaker: SYSTEM
toast_reaction.text: [i]A beat. Then Rho lifts his mug. Then Vesper. Then, one by one, they all do — even Jalen, who raises his coffee cup without looking up from the datapad, which is its own kind of ceremony.[/i]

player_story_mine.speaker: SYSTEM
player_story_mine.text: [i]The table goes quiet. Not because you demanded it — because they gave it. Freely.[/i]

choices.text: Tell them about the old miner who sang while he worked — same song, every shift, for twelve years
Tell them about the time you stole extra rations to feed someone too sick to work
Tell them about the stars you could see from Shaft 7 — one crack in the rock, one sliver of sky

mine_story_singer.speaker: Avyanna
mine_story_singer.text: There was this man in Shaft 4. Kaed. He sang the same song every shift. Twelve years. Nobody knew the words. I don't think he did either, by the end. But the rhythm — it kept us moving. When he died, the shaft went quiet. And the next shift... someone else started humming it.

mine_story_rations.speaker: Avyanna
mine_story_rations.text: I stole rations once. For a woman named Sera. She was too sick to make quota, and if you didn't make quota, you didn't eat. I hid bread in my sleeve for a week. She never knew it was me. [i]A pause.[/i] She got better. Made quota again. That was enough.

mine_story_stars.speaker: Avyanna
mine_story_stars.text: Shaft 7 had a crack. Just a fracture in the rock — maybe a meter long. But at the right angle, at the right time, you could see stars through it. One thin line of sky. I used to press my face against the stone and just... look. [i]A beat.[/i] I didn't know the names of any of them. I do now.

mine_story_reaction.speaker: SYSTEM
mine_story_reaction.text: [i]No one speaks for a moment. Rho's grin is gone — replaced by something unguarded. Nyx's hands are still. Even Jalen's datapad has gone dark.[/i]

ask_origin.speaker: SYSTEM
ask_origin.text: [i]The question lands like a stone in still water. Glances exchanged — quick, coded.[/i]

vesper_origin.speaker: Vesper
vesper_origin.text: [i]Choosing words like a surgeon choosing instruments.[/i] Separately. Badly. In ways none of us planned. [i]A glance around the table.[/i] Rho was the second. Jalen was the third. Elia and Elisira came as a pair. Nyx was last, before you.

rho_origin_joke.speaker: Rho
rho_origin_joke.text: I blew up the wrong building and Vesper hired me because, and I quote, 'anyone who can make that big a mistake and survive has useful instincts.'

vesper_origin_correction.speaker: Vesper
vesper_origin_correction.text: I said you had 'chaotic competence.' There's a difference.

jalen_origin.speaker: Jalen
jalen_origin.text: [i]Not volunteering his own story. The datapad glows.[/i] We all had reasons to leave wherever we were. This ship was better than the alternative. For all of us.

nyx_quiet.speaker: Nyx
nyx_quiet.text: You're quiet tonight. More than usual. [i]Gentle.[/i] That wasn't criticism. Just observation. You're allowed to be quiet.

text_variants.text: You've been watching. I noticed. [i]Gentle.[/i] That's not criticism. Observation is how we learn. You're doing fine.
Good observation earlier. About the Compact patrols. [i]Gentle.[/i] You're thinking like crew already. That's faster than most.
That toast. [i]Gentle.[/i] It meant something. To them. To me. You should know that.

choices.text: 'I'm just listening. Learning how this works.'
'Can I help with dishes? I know it's not my turn, but...'
Just smile. The first real one in weeks.

nyx_strategy.speaker: Nyx
nyx_strategy.text: Good strategy. You'll figure out when you want to talk. No pressure.

rho_grace.speaker: Rho
rho_grace.text: [i]Waving you off.[/i] You're still new. Grace period. First month on crew, you're exempt from chores. Your job is learning to exist here without constant survival math.

vesper_explains.speaker: Vesper
vesper_explains.text: Lets you acclimate without added pressure. You'll contribute plenty. Right now, just be here.

smile_end.speaker: SYSTEM
smile_end.text: [i]You smile. Small. Involuntary. Rho notices and grins back. Jalen nods without comment. The presence behind your eyes pulses — warm, approving, curious about this thing called 'belonging.'[/i]

tone_shift.speaker: SYSTEM
tone_shift.text: [i]The meal is winding. Bowls are empty, coffee is dregs. The warmth of the galley has that fragile quality — like a candle flame in a room with too many doors.[/i]

jalen_tone_shift.speaker: Jalen
jalen_tone_shift.text: [i]Setting down the datapad. The first time all evening.[/i] There's something else. The Kael-7 patrols... they correlate with a Writ-Bound sighting report. Unconfirmed, but the source is solid.

table_silence.speaker: SYSTEM
table_silence.text: [i]The table goes still. Not quiet — still. The kind of stillness that has weight. Rho stops smiling. Elia's hand moves to her belt, instinctive, then back. Elisira's eyes find Vesper's.[/i]

vesper_writ_response.speaker: Vesper
vesper_writ_response.text: [i]Very calm. The kind of calm that is itself a warning.[/i] How solid?

jalen_writ_detail.speaker: Jalen
jalen_writ_detail.text: Seven out of ten. The reporter has been reliable before. Never cried wolf on Writ-Bound specifically.

elia_writ_response.speaker: Elia
elia_writ_response.text: [i]Flat. Professional.[/i] Then we assume it's real until proven otherwise. What's our exposure?

jalen_exposure.speaker: Jalen
jalen_exposure.text: Two jumps from our planned route. If they're moving toward Kael-7, our window narrows. If they're moving away, we're fine.

danger_response_choice.speaker: SYSTEM
danger_response_choice.text: [i]The warmth of dinner hasn't left the room. But something else has entered it. The meal and the mission, occupying the same air.[/i]

choices.text: [Tactics DC 12] 'Two jumps means a day's warning at minimum. Enough to adjust.'
[Insight DC 12] Watch the crew's reactions — read what they're not saying
'What are Writ-Bound?'
Stay quiet. You don't know enough to speak.

tactics_danger_success.speaker: Vesper
tactics_danger_success.text: [i]A nod — slower this time, weighted.[/i] Correct. A day's buffer is workable. Jalen, set up an alert threshold. If Writ-Bound signatures enter single-jump range, I want to know immediately.

tactics_danger_failure.speaker: Jalen
tactics_danger_failure.text: [i]Measured.[/i] Two jumps is variable. Depends on their drive configuration. Could be a day. Could be six hours. Don't assume comfort margins.

insight_danger_success.speaker: SYSTEM
insight_danger_success.text: [i]Rho's fear is in his hands — they've gone still, the demolitions expert's instinct to hold steady when the ground shifts. Jalen's fear is in his voice — too flat, too precise. Nyx isn't afraid. She's calculating. And Vesper... Vesper is already three moves ahead, and doesn't like what they see.[/i]

insight_danger_failure.speaker: SYSTEM
insight_danger_failure.text: [i]The crew's reactions are guarded. Whatever Writ-Bound means to them, they've practiced not showing it.[/i]

writ_bound_explain.speaker: Vesper
writ_bound_explain.text: [i]Choosing each word.[/i] Writ-Bound are... Compact enforcement taken to its theological conclusion. Augmented. Indoctrinated. They don't patrol — they hunt. And their definition of 'heresy' is broader than the Compact's official line. If they're near Kael-7, it means someone drew attention.

nyx_writ_addition.speaker: Nyx
nyx_writ_addition.text: [i]Very quiet.[/i] They don't negotiate. They don't take prisoners. And they have technology that shouldn't exist. [i]A beat.[/i] I've seen their work. In a lab. What was left.

elia_brings_back.speaker: SYSTEM
elia_brings_back.text: [i]The silence stretches. The ship hums. Someone's bowl scrapes against the table — too loud in the quiet.[/i]

elia_anchor.speaker: Elia
elia_anchor.text: [i]Setting both hands flat on the table. Deliberate. An anchor.[/i] We eat. We rest. Tomorrow we plan. That's how this works. The danger will still be there in the morning. It doesn't get our evening too.

elia_anchor_reaction.speaker: SYSTEM
elia_anchor_reaction.text: [i]The words land. Not like an order — like a door closing gently on a cold wind. Rho exhales. Jalen picks up the datapad, then sets it down again. Deliberately. Nyx's shoulders ease.[/i]

elisira_anchor_support.speaker: Elisira
elisira_anchor_support.text: [i]Pouring the last of the coffee.[/i] Elia's right. Tomorrow's problems belong to tomorrow's crew. Tonight we're just people who had a meal together. That matters more than any of you want to admit.

vesper_final_word.speaker: Vesper
vesper_final_word.text: [i]Standing. Pushing in their chair — the small formality that means 'meeting adjourned.'[/i] Agreed. Early start tomorrow. Get some rest. All of you.

meal_end.speaker: SYSTEM
meal_end.text:
[i]Rho washes dishes. Jalen dries. Nyx disappears to her lab. Elia and Elisira sit close, speaking in low voices about tomorrow's work.

And you sit at the table, in a chair that's comfortable, with a full stomach, in a room full of people who aren't keeping score.

This is what dinner is supposed to be. You're only just learning that.[/i]
